strategy_summary: The bodies of Alabama cavefish allow them to survive without vision via elaborate appendages and beefed-up nerve centers. “Instead of vision, many [troglobites] have elaborate appendages and beefed-up nerve centers to interpret slight air-pressure or temperature changes, sounds, and smells.
mechanism:
The bodies of Alabama cavefish allow them to survive without vision via elaborate appendages and beefed-up nerve centers.

“Instead of vision, many [troglobites] have elaborate appendages and beefed-up nerve centers to interpret slight air-pressure or temperature changes, sounds, and smells. This sensory equipment lets them travel, sense objects moving or still, ambush prey, and according to a recent study of troglobitic fish, judge the size and suitability of prospective mates, sight unseen.” (Krajick 2007:139)
function_addressed: sense chemicals (odor, taste, etc.) from the environment
sense sound and other vibrations from the environment
sense temperature cues from the environment
